creator: apeiron@comcast.net
text:
On Tue, Jul 11, 2006 at 15:20:42 EDT, Edwin Groothuis scribbled these
curious markings:
> Maintainer of devel/p5-Module-Pluggable-Ordered,
> 
> Please note that PR ports/100103 has just been submitted.
> 
> If it contains a patch for an upgrade, an enhancement or a bug fix
> you agree on, reply to this email stating that you approve the patch
> and a committer will take care of it.
> 
> The full text of the PR can be found at:
>     http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=ports/100103

I don't know the smart guy who uploaded that version to CPAN,
but he needs a raise. *grin*

Sure, go ahead and commit it. 

Best regards,
Christopher Nehren

-- 
I abhor a system designed for the "user", if that word is a coded
pejorative meaning "stupid and unsophisticated". -- Ken Thompson
If you ask questions of idiots, you get "Joel on Software".
Unix is user friendly. However, it isn't idiot friendly.
